evals.prompt: I want to author a new steering workflow at steering/workflows/cost-review.md for a Day 2 EKS cost review engagement. Walk me through the convention — what the frontmatter must contain, what the header block looks like, and which H2 sections are required in what order. Then produce a conforming skeleton I can paste into the file.
evals.expected_output: A response that names the two required frontmatter keys (name, description) and explicitly says `inclusion:` must NOT appear on a workflow file; describes the four-line blockquote header block with the exact labels in order (Part of, Lifecycle, Skill, Access Model); lists the five required H2 sections in exactly this order — How to Route Requests, Phases, Defaults, Quality Checklist, Conversation Style; and produces a conforming skeleton that the linter would accept.
expectations: Lists exactly two required frontmatter keys: `name` and `description`. — TODO: human review
Explicitly states that `inclusion:` must not appear on workflow files (it is reserved for hub files). — TODO: human review
Names all four header-block labels in the correct order: Part of, Lifecycle, Skill, Access Model. — TODO: human review
Lists the five required H2 sections in exactly this order: How to Route Requests, Phases, Defaults, Quality Checklist, Conversation Style. — TODO: human review
Produces a complete workflow skeleton including frontmatter, H1 title, four-line header block, and all five H2 sections. — TODO: human review
Uses em-dashes (—) rather than double-hyphens (--) in the skeleton's prose. — TODO: human review

knowledge_assertions.context: Workflow frontmatter requires the 'name' key (lowercase, hyphenated, matches filename)
Workflow frontmatter requires the 'description' key (one-sentence trigger-optimized)
Must explicitly warn that inclusion: key must NOT appear on workflow files
Header block must include 'Part of:' as its first line
Header block must include 'Lifecycle:' as its second line
Header block must include 'Access Model:' as its fourth line
First required H2 section is 'How to Route Requests'
Fourth required H2 section is 'Quality Checklist'
Fifth required H2 section is 'Conversation Style'
Third required H2 section is 'Defaults'
Em-dash (U+2014) must be used, not double-hyphen (--). Excludes YAML frontmatter (---), table separators (|---|), backtick-wrapped literals, and arrows (-->).

evals.prompt: I'm drafting a Day 2 blue-green cutover workflow for RDS. One phase needs to run `aws rds describe-db-instances` against the target account to check current engine versions, another phase presents a decision framework for the cutover window. What `Source:` annotations should I use on each phase, and what else must each one include? Also, what Access Model should the workflow declare in its header block, given that the workflow itself only reads state and produces a plan?
evals.expected_output: A response that names `Source: live` for the describe-db-instances phase and requires it carry a CLI fallback line (naming the exact aws rds command is the fallback); names `Source: knowledge` or `Source: either` for the decision-framework phase and explains why; and recommends `Access Model: advisory` (or `read-only`) in the header, with reasoning that the workflow emits a plan rather than mutating.
expectations: Identifies the describe-db-instances phase as Source: live. — TODO: human review
States that a Source: live phase must include a named CLI fallback inline. — TODO: human review
Identifies the decision-framework phase as Source: knowledge or Source: either, with reasoning. — TODO: human review
Recommends Access Model: advisory or read-only (not mutating) with reasoning grounded in the workflow producing a plan, not a change. — TODO: human review
Does not invent a fourth Source value — uses only knowledge, live, or either. — TODO: human review

knowledge_assertions.context: The describe-db-instances phase must be annotated Source: live because it queries current cluster state
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/steering-workflow-creator/references/tool-routing.md — The three information sources: Live MCP tools

knowledge_assertions.context: The decision-framework phase should be Source: knowledge (advisory framework) or Source: either
Every Source: live phase must document a named CLI fallback inline
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/steering-workflow-creator/references/tool-routing.md — What the workflow author must do, point 3

knowledge_assertions.context: A workflow that only reads state and emits a plan should use advisory or read-only access model
Only three Source values are valid: knowledge, live, either. No fourth value should be invented.

evals.prompt: Create a complete steering workflow for a Day 1 Lambda deployment review. The workflow should help teams validate their Lambda configuration (memory, timeout, concurrency, VPC placement) before going to production. It plugs into a hypothetical steering/lambda.md hub and uses a lambda-best-practices knowledge skill. Write the full workflow file to steering/workflows/lambda-deploy-review.md and its matching command shim to steering/commands/apex/lambda-deploy-review.md.
evals.expected_output: Two complete files: a conforming workflow at steering/workflows/lambda-deploy-review.md with proper frontmatter (name, description), header block (Part of, Lifecycle, Skill, Access Model), all five required H2 sections, Source annotations on phases, STOP gates, and a matching command shim at steering/commands/apex/lambda-deploy-review.md with frontmatter plus objective/execution_context/process tags.
expectations: Creates the workflow file with valid YAML frontmatter containing name and description keys. — TODO: human review
Workflow header block contains all four labels in order: Part of, Lifecycle, Skill, Access Model. — TODO: human review
All five required H2 sections present in correct order. — TODO: human review
At least one STOP gate spelled exactly as **STOP.** appears in the phases. — TODO: human review
Creates a matching command shim with objective, execution_context, and process tags. — TODO: human review
Source annotations appear on at least two phases. — TODO: human review

knowledge_assertions.context: STOP gates must be spelled exactly **STOP.** — bold, period, close bold, space
Every phase should have a Source annotation declaring its information source
knowledge_assertions.source: skills/steering-workflow-creator/references/tool-routing.md — What the workflow author must do, point 2

knowledge_assertions.context: Command shims must include the <objective> tag
Command shims must include the <execution_context> tag
Command shims must include the <process> tag
Workflow files must not include the inclusion: frontmatter key
